channel.title: Hack it until you learn it
channel.description: Lessons learned along the way. Experiences building java and cross platform c , c++ libraries.

item.title: A new cross platform cpp IDE
item.description:
Today Jetbrains announced EAP with a blog post for their new shiny C/C++ IDE. Lets try it.

C-lion name sea lion logo , I see what you did there ;).

I am a fan of jetbeans. I am using intellij idea for java and I was using AppCode for C++ development. Appcode is also a jetbrains product. It is originally for objective-c but it also supports C++/C. But it has some problems with C++. Here is some problems with it before we look into new product:

  • It sometimes had problems with finding where a method is used.

  • It is based on XCode. Although it enables to make detailed configurations from Xcode UI, it makes the IDE only available on Mac. Which is a bummer, since I need IDE for my linux and windows environment too.

  • It has a weird default behaviour when generating methods like getters and setters or when overriding methods. Even tough I try to generate the getter and setter in source file, it insists on generating them in header files. And sometimes it puts the generated code in wrong place and code ends up in a non compilable state.

  • Although version control support was working fine with a straight forward Xcode project, when I switch to CMake generated Xcode project, VCS failed to work. At the end, I could not integrate it properly wit CMake.

Lets see how C-lion is doing compared to these. First successes:

  • It has builtin CMake support. A huge plus for me since I am using CMake with my projects. I just opened a new project with my CMakeLists.txt and it works.

  • It is cross platform. It means I can work with one IDE in mac, linux and Windows. Or am I? I need to test this on my Windows machine. I will update this part when I did.

  • Github integration is working fine out of the box.

  • It has all the shortcuts as a usual Jetbrains product does.

Now failures:

  • It still has same weird behaviour when I try to generate methods in source file.

  • It still has problems for finding references. It looks like problem related to templated classes. —EDIT— This issue is resolved. It seems that I need to add headers also to CMakeLists.txt even tough compiler does not require it. Fair enough, since it does not break anything to add headers to configurations.

Project seems promising. Since they are in Early Access stage, I am sure that problems will be fixed. I better take visit to their issue tracker.

item.title: A dark themed blog with jekkyl
item.description:
Hi everyone, I am starting my first blog with this one. I will be writing a new post when I learned anything new , find a new good product or figure out to deal with a difficult problem. All these will be related to software, c, c++, java of course.

First thing I learned is how to build a blog with jekyll, hence the title. I came across github io pages in a couple of blogs that I was following. And heard that one of my coworkers is also using it and decided to give it a try. I am pleased with it so far. Writing blog with a familiar syntax(markdown) and publishing your posts with git push is really convenient. I don’t need any other tool or website to use my blog …. after the first setup.

 Static Website Generator: Jekyll

I am using jekyll to generate my blogs. I will skip basics on building blog with jekyyl. It has already a nice website and a quick easy setup. Here is a link for the ones who are interested in basics.

Theme

I searched a little bit on the web for a theme with black background. Why a dark theme? Well, I am a hacker. My IDE’s, my text editor, my desktop background is all with black theme. And I want my blog to look same.

And I found midnight theme for jekyll by mattgraham. You can find the original work from here. Thanks mattgraham for his work. I really like the colours and design. All I had to do is make it blog friendly. You can find latest changes I made from here.

Comments

I also added comments to my blog. I used disqus for this. It has integrations with common social plugins like facebook, google plus and it looks good with my dark theme.
